A [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] astronomical object, '''Thomork''' was the site of an [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]]. Shortly before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the facility produced [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]]. [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] before [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], the Empire spread the rumor that the Thomork shipyard was closed due to a [[hive virus]] outbreak and, to lend credence to the rumor, [[Death/Legends|killed]] over 450 people there. The shipyard was then secretly re-opened and used for Imperial construction projects such as the [[Silencer-1|first]] [[World Devastator]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]].

==Description==
Thomork was an astronomical object<ref name="DES" /> located in the [[Thomork system/Legends|Thomork system]], a part of the [[Southern Core]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA Thomork" />

==History==
[[File:WorldDevastator-NEGWT.png|thumb|left|200px|Thomork served as the secret construction site of the first World Devastator.]]
Shortly before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="EGTW" /> Thomork was the site of an [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]]<ref name="DES" /> the output of which was limited to [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="EGTW" /> It was also the site of a [[Colonist/Legends|colony]].<ref name="DES" />

[[Standard year/Legends|Years]] prior to<ref name="DES" /> [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' describes the state of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] during the events of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] series, which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]].</ref> the Empire spread the rumor that the shipyard at Thomork had been closed down due to an outbreak of<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|238|Thomork}}</ref> a contagious new [[Disease/Legends|strain]] of [[hive virus]].<ref name="DES" /> To add credence to the rumor, Imperial agents also [[Death/Legends|killed]] more than 450 of Thomork's inhabitants.<ref name="CSWE" />

The colony was abandoned, and the Thomork [[Star system/Legends|system]] took on the reputation of a massacre site, eventually becoming as infamous as the [[Dagobah system/Legends|Dagobah system]]<ref name="DES" /> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> In fact, the entire facility at Thomork was undamaged, and the Empire clandestinely re-opened the shipyard,<ref name="DES" /> taking it over for top-secret construction projects such as the ''[[Silencer-1]]'',<ref name="CSWE" /> the first of the Empire's [[World Devastator]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapons]].<ref name="DES" />

==Behind the scenes==
Thomork was first mentioned in the [[1993]] ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'', which was authored by [[Michael Allen Horne]] and published by [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="DES" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Thomork system, and therefore the celestial body Thomork, in grid square L-12.<ref name="TEA Thomork" />

On a map included in the [[2012]] book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', Thomork was erroneously labeled as "Tomark."<ref name="EGTW" /> ''The Essential Atlas'' had previously introduced that proper noun as the name of a [[Tomark system/Legends|star system]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]],<ref name="TEA" /> building off the name of the<ref name="Journal11">{{JournalCite|11|The Ando Project}}</ref> [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="IH">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''</ref> [[Tomark II]], which had been introduced in the "[[The Ando Project]]," a [[roleplaying game]] adventure published in [[November]] [[1996]] in the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]''{{'s}} [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|eleventh issue]].<ref name="Journal11" />
